findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

description: Fetch ALL beyts between two endpoints, inclusive, grouped by section. Use this — NOT many calls to get_section or lookup — when a user asks for a full story, a long passage, or a sequence of beyts spanning multiple sections. Endpoints are canonical citations like 'M2:2608' and 'M2:2775', or global i

description.name: find_sections
description.description: Find sections (stories) by TITLE. Exact words first (phrase, then AND-of-tokens); when NO title contains the words, falls back to MEANING matches in any language (hits carry match='meaning' + a cosine score — verify with get_section before relying on one). Each match includes `first_citation` and `l

description.name: explain
description.description: Get commentary (Persian + English) for a specific beyt of the Masnavi, attributed to a primary source (e.g. Abdolkarim Soroush's lectures). Returns the beyt text along with structured commentary entries. Each entry has source, author, language, body markdown, and confidence ('ai-draft' / 'reviewed'

description.name: search_divan
description.description: Full-text search across the 34,603 beyts of the ghazals of the Divan-e Shams (Divan-e Kabir) — Rumi's lyric work, distinct from the Masnavi. Returns beyts with divan citations (G{ghazal}:{beyt}, e.g. G1393:1). Use when you know the exact Persian words; use search_divan_meaning for a theme in any lan

description.name: search_divan_meaning
description.description: Semantic search over the Divan-e Shams ghazals: given a theme, feeling, or idea in ANY language, returns the GHAZALS closest in meaning — each with its best-matching couplet as a preview and a cosine score. Both couplet-level and whole-ghazal vectors are searched, so a ghazal whose overall theme mat

description.name: get_ghazal
description.description: Fetch ONE complete ghazal of the Divan-e Shams by its canonical number (1..3230) or citation ('G1393' / 'G1393:2'). Returns every beyt plus prev/next ghazal numbers. Famous example: get_ghazal(1393) = «مرده بدم زنده شدم…».

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.
